prompt:
An entry-level FDM 3D printer with a 220×220×250 mm build volume sits idle, its print head hovering above the bed with a visible blob of hardened filament at the nozzle tip and a rough, failed first layer on the build surface. The rest of the printer frame and bed look clean, but the nozzle clearly needs cleaning before another print. The control panel shows a neutral or error state without readable text, emphasizing that the issue is mechanical, not software.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk maker space with soft, even lighting, neatly organized tools, and spare nozzles and cleaning brushes in the blurred background. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable markings on the printer, and a composition that centers the clogged nozzle, messy filament at the hotend, and imperfect first layer while the rest of the machine remains softly out of focus, illustrating a common 3D printing maintenance scenario.
